ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER IN PART AND OVERRULING DEMURRER IN PART

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF SOFIA NEW AND JOSEPH NEWS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TEN (10) DAYS TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, CONSTRUCTIVE FRAUD, TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING A CONFIDENTIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND THE SEVERE OR SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DEFENDANTS SUFFERED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING DEFENDANTS SUFFERED SEVERE OR SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS' PROTESTATIONS THAT DEFENDANTS ARE IN FACT THE FRAUDULENT PARTIES REPRESENT A DISPUTE OF FACT NOT RESOLVABLE ON DEMURRER. SIMILARLY, DEFENDANTS' CROSS-COMPLAINT PLEADS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM AND BY WHAT MEANS THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE, AND THE EXISTENCE OF A DUTY BASED UPON THE PARTIES' LONGTIME FRIENDSHIP AND BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE CLAIMS FOR MISREPRESENTATION AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS RELATED TO P'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS THAT THE LOAN WOULD BE REPAID. CAUSES OF ACTION 7 THROUGH 10 ARE PROPER AS DERIVATIVES OF THE OTHER PROPERLY PLEAD CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SUBMITTED, SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: R. GOULDING; NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of Sofia New And Joseph New With Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George Hill of Rodriguez Law Group telephone number 626-888-5206 for Plaintiffs Sofia New and Joseph New.
Thomas Eastridge of Law Offices of Thomas Eastridge telephone number 510-530-1700 for Defendant Aurora Langit.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MANUEL LANGIT and AURORA LANGIT'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Sofia New And Joseph New With Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions is granted in part. Within twenty days, Ms. New must make herself available for a further session of her deposition to be scheduled at her convenience and the convenience of counsel that must not exceed five hours in duration. At least two days prior to that session, Ms. New must produce copies of all non-privileged documents in her possession, custody and control that are requested in the deposition notice that have not been previously produced. Ms. New must also bring originals of all documents that she produced to the next session of her deposition. If within three days counsel for the News confirms in writing that Mr. New will not testify at the trial because of his diminished cognitive ability, Mr. New's deposition may proceed only by way of written questions per CCP 2028.010 et seq except that all days provided for in those sections will be divided by three (e.g. 30 days in 2028.030 (b) is 10 days). If counsel for the News does not confirm in writing that Mr. New will not testify at the trial, then Mr. New must make himself available for an oral deposition within twenty days at his convenience and the convenience of counsel and that deposition must not exceed three hours in duration. The Langits' request for monetary sanctions is denied. The Langits failed to establish the need for an additional session of Ms. New's deposition and she agreed to be deposed for an additional five hours. Nor have the Langits presented any evidence or any suggestion contradicting the assertion that Mr. New has severely diminished cognitive ability due to having suffered from a stroke. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Housing Court Law and Motion's Calendar for Monday April 20, 2015 Line 8.

PLAINTIFF SOFIA NEW AND JOSEPH NEWS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

Appearances:Sevag Simonian,The Rodriguez Law Group 626-888-5206 appearing via Courtcall for Plaintiff, Sofia New; Thomas Eastridge, Law Offices of Thomas Eastridge 510-530

Both sides submit to the tentative ruling:
The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF SOFIA NEW AND JOSEPH NEWS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.
The Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave TEN (10) days to amend as to the second cause of action, constructive fraud, to plead facts showing a confidential relationship between the parties. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 5, intentional infliction of emotional distress, with leave to amend to plead facts showing outrageous conduct and the severe or serious emotional distress Defendants suffered. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 6, negligent infliction of emotional distress, with leave to amend to plead facts showing Defendants suffered severe or serious emotional distress.
The Demurrer is OVERRULED as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs' protestations that Defendants are in fact the fraudulent parties represent a dispute of fact not resolvable on demurrer. Similarly, Defendants' cross-complaint pleads with sufficient particularity how, when, where, to whom and by what means the alleged misrepresentations were made, and the existence of a duty based upon the parties' longtime friendship and business relationship sufficient to support the claims for misrepresentation and negligent infliction of emotional distress related to P's alleged misrepresentations that the loan would be repaid. Causes of action 7 through 10 are proper as derivatives of the other properly plead causes of action.
Order submitted, signed and filed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: R. Goulding; Not Reported.
